info.title: Advanced Web Development Quiz
info.description: Test your skills in HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Flask concepts. Aim for 75% to pass. Best of luck!

questions.question: What is the primary role of the HTML `<head>` element?
choices: To display the main content of a webpage
To contain metadata and links to resources like stylesheets and scripts
questions.answer: To contain metadata and links to resources like stylesheets and scripts
questions.justification: The `<head>` element is crucial for including metadata, linking CSS files, JavaScript scripts, and other resources.

questions.question: Which CSS property is used to make a webpage responsive?
questions.justification: Media queries allow CSS styles to adapt based on screen size, orientation, and other device properties, making designs responsive.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `async` attribute in a `<script>` tag?
choices: To load the script after all content has loaded
To load the script simultaneously with other resources
To delay the script execution until the user interacts
To preload the script without executing it
questions.answer: To load the script simultaneously with other resources
questions.justification: The `async` attribute allows a script to load in parallel with other resources, improving page load speed.

questions.question: In Flask, what does the `@app.route` decorator do?
choices: Defines a route or URL endpoint for a view function
Creates a new Flask application instance
questions.answer: Defines a route or URL endpoint for a view function
questions.justification: The `@app.route` decorator maps a URL to a specific view function, allowing Flask to handle requests to that URL.

questions.question: Which JavaScript method is used to select an element by its class name?
questions.justification: The `getElementsByClassName` method selects elements with a specific class name.

questions.question: What is the default HTTP method used by a Flask form submission?
questions.justification: By default, forms use the GET method unless specified otherwise in the `method` attribute.

questions.question: What does the CSS `z-index` property control?
choices: The position of an element relative to its container
The stacking order of overlapping elements
questions.answer: The stacking order of overlapping elements
questions.justification: The `z-index` property specifies the stack order of elements, determining which elements appear on top.

questions.question: What is the role of the Flask `render_template` function?
choices: To render HTML templates with dynamic data
questions.answer: To render HTML templates with dynamic data
questions.justification: `render_template` is used to render HTML files and pass dynamic data from Python to the templates.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `position: absolute` CSS property?
choices: To position an element relative to its closest positioned ancestor
To center an element on the page
To make an element float next to text
To align elements in a flexbox container
questions.answer: To position an element relative to its closest positioned ancestor
questions.justification: `position: absolute` positions an element based on the nearest ancestor with a position other than static.

questions.question: Which Flask function is used to handle 404 errors?
questions.justification: The `@app.errorhandler` decorator defines custom responses for HTTP error codes, including 404.

questions.question: What does the CSS `flex` property define in a flex container?
choices: The growth, shrinkage, and base size of an item
The alignment of items along the main axis
questions.answer: The growth, shrinkage, and base size of an item
questions.justification: The `flex` property is shorthand for setting `flex-grow`, `flex-shrink`, and `flex-basis`.

questions.question: Which JavaScript method stops an event from propagating to parent elements?
questions.justification: `stopPropagation` prevents the event from bubbling up to parent elements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of Flask's `url_for` function?
choices: To generate URLs for static files
To create dynamic URLs for view functions
questions.answer: To create dynamic URLs for view functions
questions.justification: `url_for` generates URLs dynamically based on view function names and arguments, ensuring maintainability.

questions.question: Which HTML element is used to include JavaScript code directly on a webpage?
questions.justification: The `<script>` element is used to include or link JavaScript code.

questions.question: How do you specify a route parameter in Flask?
choices: Using square brackets in the URL
Using curly braces in the URL
Using angle brackets in the URL
questions.answer: Using angle brackets in the URL
questions.justification: Flask uses angle brackets to define dynamic route parameters, e.g., `/user/<username>`.
